status: exact corrected finite coercivity target for the critical scaled Newman layer; not a proof of its sign, Lambda<=0, or RH
proof_boundary: This artifact proves the uncorrected component identity and the exact curvature contribution of the published Riemann-Siegel correction. It identifies the refined remainder order and the live finite target. It does not prove the corrected coercivity inequality, positivity throughout the critical layer, positivity for all x and t, Lambda<=0, or RH.

exact.refined_remainder: H_t/A_t=J_(N,t)+r_ref; the published e_C replaces the e_C0 factor 1 by tilde_epsilon(s_-)+tilde_epsilon(s_+)=O(1/N)
exact.shrinking_collar: On radius rho=1/L collars, the refined normalized C2 remainder is o(L^2) after including one adjacent-N correction
exact.live_target: Prove C_t[J_(N,t)] greater than its explicit refined C2 remainder budget uniformly for 0<=t*L<=c_*+o(1)

rows.claim: The remaining asymptotic obstruction is a bounded scaled-time finite Riemann-Siegel problem.
rows.proof_boundary: The c>c_*+epsilon region is closed by the oscillatory zeta handoff theorem.

rows.claim: The finite-main Laguerre curvature has an exact component formula valid even when D vanishes.
rows.proof_boundary: No division by D and no zero-free assumption.

rows.claim: The leading mixed phase term is invariant under the normalizer rotation.
Polymath 15 supplies an explicit leading Riemann-Siegel endpoint correction.
rows.proof_boundary: Imported from the published A+B-C approximation.

rows.claim: The correct critical finite main object includes the normalized endpoint correction.
rows.proof_boundary: Omitting Q loses one asymptotic order in the critical layer.

rows.claim: The endpoint correction enters the finite curvature through an explicit six-term perturbation.
After extracting Q, the published contour remainder gains a full factor of the saddle index.
rows.formula: H_t/A_t=J_(N,t)+r_ref; the published e_C replaces the e_C0 factor 1 by tilde_epsilon(s_-)+tilde_epsilon(s_+)=O(1/N)
rows.proof_boundary: Uses the published tilde-epsilon bound; derivative constants remain to be made explicit.

rows.claim: A scale-adapted Cauchy collar keeps normalizer growth bounded while differentiating the refined remainder.
rows.formula: On radius rho=1/L collars, the refined normalized C2 remainder is o(L^2) after including one adjacent-N correction
rows.proof_boundary: Asymptotic transfer schema; an explicit finite threshold is not supplied here.

rows.claim: The global tail is reduced to one corrected finite oscillatory coercivity inequality.
rows.formula: Prove C_t[J_(N,t)] greater than its explicit refined C2 remainder budget uniformly for 0<=t*L<=c_*+o(1)
rows.proof_boundary: Open and RH-level; no sign is asserted.

rows.claim: Absolute coefficient bounds stop at c=4; the exponent-pair handoff lowers the open boundary to c_*, but neither argument certifies the corrected coercivity at or below c_*.
rows.proof_boundary: Cancellation, not additional truncation depth, is required.
